item.title: This is the Ukrainian soldier Oleksii, callsign "Botanik." He spent 343 days on the front lines without rotation .. voluntarily.
item.description: CNN reports on the story of 37-year-old Ukrainian Ground Forces infantry officer Oleksii, callsign "Botanik." He served in the Zaporizhzhia direction for almost a year, from April 1, 2025, to March 8, 2026. The publication states that he stayed on the front voluntarily due to a shortage of personnel in his unit: "There are not enough people in my company, and about half of the soldiers are over 50 years old. Ideally, it's one month on the position, one month of recovery, but right now that is unrealistic." After 343 days, he was given a short leave. He celebrated his daughter's 10th birthday, taught her to ride a bicycle, and then returned to his unit.
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: The Most Trans Friendly Country?
item.description: I finally talk Japan! This morning at 7:30 am PST live chat with me as TylerfromtheInternet and I dive into travel culture, being tall, trans bathroom politics (finally?), and why traveling as a trans woman outside the U.S. can feel so freeing! https://youtu.be/JVb4zmQl1pA
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.dc:creator: CrimsonPermanentAssurance

item.title: Sometimes Modern Problems Require Traditional Solutions
item.description: A simple DIY that uses basic chemistry to power a small LED. When you soak metal strips in saltwater, the mixture acts like an electrolyte and creates a tiny flow of electricity that can light up low-power devices. How bright it gets depends on the type of metals you use (video used copper & galvanized iron), the salt concentration, and how fresh the solution is. More ions + cleaner metals = stronger and more stable light. Safety note: This isn't endless energy - the metals slowly corrode and the solution (electrolyte) weakens over time.
item.dc:creator: TheLastSpaceman

item.title: GovBrief Today #445
item.description: The first man walked up to Rumeysa Ozturk like he was going to ask for directions. He was the point man on an arrest team that whisked her off a Somerville, Massachusetts street for co-authoring a student op-ed. After 45 days in a Louisiana cell, an immigration judge ruled her status had been lawful all along. The Justice Department fired that judge last week. Ozturk flew home to Turkey Friday with her PhD, on her own timeline. After her release, she told reporters she had "faith in the American system of justice." The government settled only when it knew it would lose. Call your representatives through 5 Calls and Resistbot and demand Congress bar speech-based visa revocation. We've emailed 7,000+ stories over 445 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Embedded links, data, images at the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260420-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-apr20 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/kaskida-suit [3] https://go.govbrief.today/md-feds-sue [4] https://go.govbrief.today/paxton-act-blue [5] https://go.govbrief.today/tariff-portal [6] https://go.govbrief.today/wright-wrong-trump [7] https://go.govbrief.today/ozturk-leaves [8] https://go.govbrief.today/chavez-deremer-resigns [9] https://go.govbrief.today/mejia-sworn [10] https://go.govbrief.today/ethics-misconduct [11] https://go.govbrief.today/mills-maced [12] https://go.govbrief.today/opm-med-records [13] https://go.govbrief.today/goldplus-tsa [14] https://go.govbrief.today/patel-atlantic [15] https://go.govbrief.today/pa-medicaid-abortion Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Memes vol 665
item.description:
I quoted this at work yesterday while HR was there
We’ve been doing bandage changes for this dog at work every week and every time she comes in I somehow end up helping hold her and my legs are in agony when she’s done because of the floor, the position, and how she’s laying on me
They look like curious George omg
Whole family is good at eating lead
Gertie tax. All anybody talks about with this picture is the women- those were there when he got here
Bruce tax
Big Mama tax
Nessie tax

item.title: Knights of Guinevere: Mr. Tail
item.description: Source: https://moringmark.tumblr.com/ Knights of Guinevere is a new project, created by Dana Terrace (creator of The Owl House) and John Bailey Owens (writer for The Owl House). The pilot is currently available on Glitch's channel on Youtube, here: https://youtu.be/MCAdbUaMlAE Update, the show has been greenlit: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3oC8ta-7Rds

item.title: "The MAMDANI Act" - Fuck the GOP...
item.description: https://roy.house.gov/media/press-releases/rep-roy-introduces-mamdani-act-denaturalize-and-deport-marxists-and-islamic The MAMDANI Act enacts sweeping immigration law changes that would deport, denaturalize, deny U.S. citizenship, or entry to any alien who is a member of a socialist party, a communist party, the Chinese Communist Party, Islamic fundamentalist party, advocates for socialism, communism, Marxism, or Islamic fundamentalism. Fuck the GOP, and old guard DEMs. (The ones who stood in the way of Mamdani's election because it made them look bad, and shook up things to much for their AIPAC and corporate leash holders.)
item.dc:creator: Imadethisaccounttopost

item.title: A Fine Assortment of Stolen Memes, Vol. 17
item.description:
And then not having any energy to do anything about it, that's fun
I like to think that my whimsy helps keep me employed.
I was eating a bagel and lox every morning for nearly two months. Delicious, but then I started getting tummy aches.
I used to live off those things. Now I look at one funny and I start feeling irritable.
item.dc:creator: Vercury

item.title: This quote by Charles Bukowski
item.description: This quote by Charles Bukowski is a biting critique of societal values and the unfairness of historical legacy. Bukowski argues that the most authentic, soulful, and "best" people often live and die in obscurity, neglected by a world that doesn't value their true worth. In contrast, those who claw their way to power or fame—whom he cynically labels the "worst men"—are the ones rewarded with grand monuments and public recognition. The final image of pigeons "shitting upon" these statues adds a layer of Bukowski's signature gritty humor. He suggests that even the highest social honors are ultimately absurd and meaningless in the face of time. He is reminding us that a person’s true value isn't measured by public accolades, but by the depth and honesty of their life, even if it was lived in the shadows.
item.dc:creator: Tapthisbong
